title: Appoint a trusted helper to manage your VOA digital service
body: "When to appoint a trusted helper  You need to set up a business rates valuation account to view the rateable values of your properties and report any changes to your property details.  If you cannot set up an account because you do not have the required identity documents, someone else in your organisation can set up an account for your organisation and create an assistant role for you in the account. This will allow you to sign into the account and manage your business rates.  If you are not part of an organisation or no one in your organisation can set up an account, you can appoint a trusted helper. Your trusted helper can set up an account and create an assistant role for you. This will allow you to sign into the account and manage your business rates. A trusted helper could be a friend, family member or voluntary organisation. You can only have one trusted helper at any time.  If you cannot set up an account for any other reason, find out how you can get extra support from the Valuation Office Agency (VOA). You may not need to appoint a trusted helper.  How to fill in the form  You can download the form and fill it in on-screen or print the form and fill it in.  You should fill in the form yourself. Both you and your trusted helper need to sign the form, or we will not accept it. You can sign the form digitally.              Appoint a trusted helper    MS Word Document, 53.4 KB     This file may not be suitable for users of assistive technology.    Request an accessible format.    If you use assistive technology (such as a screen reader) and need a version of this document in a more accessible format, please email different.format@hmrc.gov.uk. Please tell us what format you need. It will help us if you say what assistive technology you use.      Where to send the form  Attach the completed form to an email. Send it to ccaservice@voa.gov.uk.  If you prefer, you can send the completed form by post to:   Trusted HelperValuation Office AgencyWycliffe House Green Lane Durham DH1 3UW   After you appoint a trusted helper  The VOA will authorise your trusted helper to set up your account. We will only talk directly to your trusted helper if they need help setting up or managing your account. We will not accept any other contact from your trusted helper. Find out how to get extra support from the VOA, including if you need someone to talk to the VOA for you.  The VOA will treat all actions taken in the online account as being from the owner, occupier or ratepayer.   It is your responsibility to make sure any information submitted to the VOA on your behalf is correct. If it is not correct, you might have to pay a penalty and pay any additional rates you owe.   If you no longer want the trusted helper to have access to your account, you need to:    Ask your trusted helper to change the access code settings...
